rulings.Case Title: STEVEN PETERSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 9, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Gardner Denver, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether the gaskets he may have been exposed to that are attributable to defendant contained asbestos. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Ay¿s declaration are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LYNN R. BARANCO et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 9, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Truitt & White Lumber Co., Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication for declaratory relief against defendant DAP, Inc. is denied. The moving party failed to satisfy its burden of production that it was not negligent or otherwise at fault regarding its sale of DAP products that Ms. Baranco was exposed to. (Stonegate Homeowners Assn. v. Staben (2006) 144 Cal. App. 4th 740, 749-52). = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LYNN R. BARANCO et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 9, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant General Cable Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication on plaintiffs¿ strict liability claims is granted. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Ms. Baranco was exposed to an asbestos-containing product or material attributable to defendant. The deposition testimony of Mr. Baranco and Mr. Katz preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Strict liability does not apply to plaintiffs¿ claims against defendant since the undisputed facts establish that Ms. Baranco was not exposed to an asbestos-containing product or material attributable to defendant that was placed in the stream of commerce or that had been sold to a consumer. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LYNN R. BARANCO et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 9, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant DAP, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1-4 (exposure/causation) and granted as to issue 5 (punitive damages). Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production on the issue of whether Ms. Baranco¿s sanding of the asbestos-containing DAP products released respirable fibers. The objections to Mr. Treat¿s declaration are sustained. Plaintiffs concede the lack of merit to their request for punitive damages. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND CURRIER et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 9, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 10. Defendant University Mechanical & Engineering Contractors, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. The declaration of Thomas Hixson creates a triable issue whether Mr. Currier was exposed to asbestos-containing materials disturbed by defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Hixson¿s declaration are overruled. = (220/HEK)
